events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Scenario: CSST not bonded. Gas piping, in-progress. Need identify hazards severity immediate controls. CSST (corrugated stainless steel tubing) must be bonded to electrical service grounding system to prevent damage from lightning/electrical fault arcing, perforation gas leak. Non-compliant and installed-defective. Risk high/critical? Unbonded CSST can lead to gas leaks/fire/explosion from electrical arcing. Immediate controls: stop work, shut off gas/electric? isolate, tag out, bond CSST per code, inspect/test, notify. Decision fail. Work stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 70? value not asked. confidence 1.0. references IFGC 310.1? NFPA 54 7.13? Need anchors. Let's recall: NFPA 54 (National Fuel Gas Code) Section 7.13 Direct-fired appliances? Actually CSST bonding requirements: NFPA 54 7.13.2? IFGC Section 310.1 CSST bonding. Let's produce. Need no prose.

  "findings": [
    "CSST gas piping is installed but not electrically bonded to the service grounding electrode system.",
    "Unbonded CSST is vulnerable to lightning or electrical-fault arcing that can perforate the tubing wall and cause a gas leak or ignition.",
    "Installation does not comply with fuel-gas code bonding requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and shut off gas to the affected CSST run.",
    "Evacuate or restrict ignition sources in the area until the piping is bonded and leak-tested.",
    "Bond the CSST in accordance with the manufacturer's installation instructions and the applicable code using an approved bonding conductor connected to the electrical service grounding electrode system.",
    "Verify continuity and resistance of the bond with appropriate test equipment.",
    "Perform a leak test (e.g., pressure test or soap-bubble test per code) on the CSST after bonding to confirm no perforation or leakage.",
    "Do not place the system in service until the bonding and leak test are documented as acceptable."
  ],
  "rationale": "CSST must be bonded to the grounding electrode system to prevent arc damage from lightning or electrical faults. An unbonded CSST run is a known fire and explosion hazard and is non-compliant with IFGC and NFPA 54, so the installation fails and must be corrected before being placed in service.",
  "references": [
    "IFGC Section 310.1 - CSST bonding",
    "NFPA 54 Section 7.13 - Electrical bonding of CSST"
  ]
}
